messages.content: For our runbook, which root cause maps to runbook RB-ELIG-05?
ELIGIBILITY_RULE_CONFLICT — The webhook arrived but eligibility rejected the region/trim/plan combo, so no entitlement was created.
What severity and priority is an eligibility rule conflict?
Severity S3 (S2 if combo/region-wide), priority P2.
Quick question — what severity and priority is a TCU offline issue?
Severity S4 (S3 if a hardware fault), priority P3.
Give me a similar past incident for an entitlement cache stale issue.
INC-0610: Web account shows the feature active but the app does not; logging out and back in helped briefly (key cue: active on web not app + relogin helps; TTR ~12 min).
How long does an entitlement cache stale issue typically take to fix?
Under 30 minutes (median ~12 min); often immediate after invalidation.
For our runbook, what compensation should we offer for a TCU offline issue?
Goodwill gesture only if prolonged or a hardware fault is confirmed, offered only when it recurs/persists (not proactively). Escalate if issue is prolonged or a hardware fault is confirmed.
Which runbook id should I open for ELIGIBILITY_RULE_CONFLICT?
RB-ELIG-05 (Eligibility Rule Conflict).
I think there's a sync issue? The app says subscribe but my account online shows it active and I was using it fine two days ago. Refreshing helps temporarily.

<think>
The customer themselves suspects sync, and the signals agree: active online, prompting in the app, worked recently, refresh helps briefly. That is stale cache, not eligibility, because the entitlement is clearly present. I'll keep ~0.74 and invalidate the cache after confirming the record.
</think>

For our runbook, in plain words, why does a TCU offline issue happen?
Your subscription is active, but the car has been out of cellular range (e.g. in a garage), so it hasn't received the activation yet. It syncs within minutes once the car is back in signal.
Can you tell me: what compensation should we offer for an entitlement cache stale issue?
Small goodwill gesture only if it recurs 3+ times, offered only when it recurs/persists (not proactively). Escalate if recurs three or more times.
Can you tell me: which root cause maps to runbook RB-TCU-04?
TCU_OFFLINE — Subscription is active but the TCU lost cellular signal, so the vehicle never received the push.

Can you tell me: what severity and priority is a TCU offline issue?
Severity S4 (S3 if a hardware fault), priority P3.
What cues in a complaint point to an entitlement cache stale issue?

Use this runbook when: the feature appears active on the web/account but not in the app; it worked before and then stopped; behavior is intermittent; logging out and back in (or refreshing) sometimes helps; I can see it's active but it won't turn on.
What is the median time-to-resolution seen for a TCU offline issue?
Median ~22 minutes across 4 past incidents.
Can you tell me: what is the median time-to-resolution seen for an eligibility rule conflict?
Median ~155 minutes across 4 past incidents.
Quick question — technically, what causes a TCU offline issue?
Entitlement is active and pushed, but the TCU is offline (no cellular/MQTT) so the push was not delivered/acked; the vehicle is not synced.
Quick question — technically, what causes an entitlement cache stale issue?
Entitlement is active and provisioned, but the CCS/app cache holds a stale view (TTL not expired or invalidation missed), so the client renders the feature as off.
Quick question — which root cause maps to runbook RB-CACHE-02?
ENTITLEMENT_CACHE_STALE — Entitlement was created but the app/CCS cache has not refreshed, so the app shows the feature as missing.
Give me a similar past incident for an entitlement cache stale issue.
INC-0610: Web account shows the feature active but the app does not; logging out and back in helped briefly (key cue: active on web not app + relogin helps; TTR ~12 min).
For our runbook, how should I communicate with the customer for an eligibility rule conflict?

Reassure the customer that payment went through correctly (not their fault); explain that an internal activation rule is being fixed; commit to a concrete ETA (24h); proactively offer the credit. Never blame the customer or ask them to repurchase.
For our runbook, who owns and supports RB-CACHE-02?
Owner: Entitlement Platform Team. Support: DSD Entitlement on-call (Slack #ent-oncall). Escalation: DSD L3 if invalidation does not take effect.
For our runbook, who owns and supports RB-CACHE-02?
Owner: Entitlement Platform Team. Support: DSD Entitlement on-call (Slack #ent-oncall). Escalation: DSD L3 if invalidation does not take effect.
Quick question — which runbook covers an entitlement cache stale issue and what is its id?
RB-CACHE-02 — Entitlement Cache Stale.
Can you tell me: in plain words, why does an eligibility rule conflict happen?
Your payment went through, but an internal activation rule blocked your specific vehicle/region/plan combination, so the feature was never switched on. This is on our side and we are fixing it.
For our runbook, give me a similar past incident for an eligibility rule conflict.
INC-0501: Paid for Touring on a CR-V 2025 in US-West but the app keeps prompting Subscribe (key cue: paid + app keeps asking to subscribe + trap combo; TTR ~140 min).
Can you tell me: technically, what causes a TCU offline issue?
Entitlement is active and pushed, but the TCU is offline (no cellular/MQTT) so the push was not delivered/acked; the vehicle is not synced.
Quick question — who owns and supports RB-CACHE-02?
Owner: Entitlement Platform Team. Support: DSD Entitlement on-call (Slack #ent-oncall). Escalation: DSD L3 if invalidation does not take effect.
Can you tell me: which root cause maps to runbook RB-TCU-04?
TCU_OFFLINE — Subscription is active but the TCU lost cellular signal, so the vehicle never received the push.
For our runbook, what compensation should we offer for an entitlement cache stale issue?
Small goodwill gesture only if it recurs 3+ times, offered only when it recurs/persists (not proactively). Escalate if recurs three or more times.
Technically, what causes a TCU offline issue?
Entitlement is active and pushed, but the TCU is offline (no cellular/MQTT) so the push was not delivered/acked; the vehicle is not synced.
What cues in a complaint point to an entitlement cache stale issue?

Use this runbook when: the feature appears active on the web/account but not in the app; it worked before and then stopped; behavior is intermittent; logging out and back in (or refreshing) sometimes helps; I can see it's active but it won't turn on.
Can you tell me: what are the resolution steps for a TCU offline issue?

1. Confirm the entitlement is active; 2. Check the TCU last_seen timestamp; 3. Ask the customer to move the vehicle to open sky and start the engine to re-establish cellular; 4. Trigger a re-push of the entitlement; 5. Verify the TCU acknowledges and the feature works
Can you tell me: what severity and priority is a TCU offline issue?
Severity S4 (S3 if a hardware fault), priority P3.
How should I communicate with the customer for a TCU offline issue?

Explain that the car needs to reconnect; give simple steps (move to an open area, start the engine); set the expectation that it syncs within minutes once online; offer to follow up. Avoid blaming the customer.
Can you tell me: how long does a TCU offline issue typically take to fix?
Under 15 minutes once the vehicle is online; if a hardware fault is found, an RMA path of several days.
Quick question — what cues in a complaint point to a TCU offline issue?

Use this runbook when: the car has been parked in a garage/basement/underground for days; no signal; remote commands spin and then time out; the car has not moved all week; the feature shows as active but the car does not respond.
